name: sovereign-pm-webapp
description: Build the Sovereign PM web application (Node.js/Express backend, React frontend, Helm chart). Replaces the file-based PM system with a self-hosted product. The documentation layer (story 035) is done; this increment delivers the running app.
sprintGoal: Deliver the Sovereign PM web app — backend API, React UI, Dockerfile, and Helm chart — deployable into kind and ready to self-host sprint management.

stories.title: Sovereign PM: Node.js/Express backend
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/src/server/ — a Node.js + Express + TypeScript API server. Data model: Project, Epic, Story, AgentRun. PostgreSQL via Sequelize. Endpoints: CRUD for epics/stories, POST /api/prd/generate (outputs prd.json), POST /api/ralph/trigger (creates a Kubernetes Job running ralph.sh), GET /api/ralph/runs (list run history). Keycloak OIDC JWT validation middleware. OpenAPI spec in sovereign-pm/openapi.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: sovereign-pm/src/server/ TypeScript Express app compiles with npm run typecheck
Sequelize models for Project, Epic, Story, AgentRun in src/server/models/
npm test exits 0 with route-level tests covering /api/epics, /api/stories, /api/projects, /api/agent-runs
POST /api/prd/generate returns JSON matching prd.json schema (unit test verifies shape)
POST /api/ralph/trigger: src/server/routes/ralph.ts generates a K8s Job manifest spec; unit test verifies Job spec has correct image and command
Keycloak JWT middleware applied to all routes; unauthenticated request returns 401
npm run typecheck and npm run lint exit 0
openapi.yaml documents all endpoints with request/response schemas
stories.testPlan: npm run typecheck exits 0; npm run lint exits 0; npm test exits 0 with route-level test coverage for all endpoints in openapi.yaml; ls sovereign-pm/openapi.yaml; grep -E '(POST|GET|PUT|DELETE)' sovereign-pm/openapi.yaml lists all 8+ endpoints.

smart.notes: Specific 4: JWT middleware library not specified (keycloak-connect? passport-jwt? express-jwt?). Measurable 4: '8+ endpoints' threshold in testPlan is arbitrary; ACs are otherwise binary. Achievable 3: Full backend with 4 Sequelize models, 8+ endpoints, JWT middleware, OpenAPI spec, and route-level test coverage is ambitious for a single 3-point iteration — near the top of the budget. TimeBound 4: acceptance criteria are binary and scope is well-defined; no open-ended extensions.

stories.title: Sovereign PM: React frontend
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/src/client/ — a React + TypeScript + Vite frontend. Pages: Dashboard (all projects), Project view (epics/stories kanban), Story editor (title, description, acceptance criteria, priority, phase), PRD Generator (shows generated prd.json, copy button), Agent Runs (history of Ralph loops, pass/fail per story, timestamps). Keycloak OIDC via keycloak-js. Tailwind CSS. Clean, minimal design.
acceptanceCriteria: React + TypeScript + Vite app in sovereign-pm/src/client/
All 5 pages implemented: Dashboard, ProjectView, StoryEditor, PrdGenerator, AgentRuns — verified by ls src/client/pages/
src/client/App.tsx wraps routes in KeycloakProvider; src/client/keycloak.ts initializes keycloak-js with env-sourced OIDC config
src/client/pages/PrdGenerator.tsx exists and includes a navigator.clipboard or equivalent copy-to-clipboard handler
src/client/pages/AgentRuns.tsx exists with pass/fail status rendering for each story run
npm run typecheck and npm run lint exit 0
npm run build exits 0 (Vite production bundle succeeds)
stories.testPlan: npm run typecheck exits 0; npm run lint exits 0; npm run build exits 0; ls src/client/pages/{Dashboard,ProjectView,StoryEditor,PrdGenerator,AgentRuns}.tsx all exist.

smart.notes: Specific 4: 'Clean, minimal design' is subjective; state management approach and API client library are unspecified. Measurable 4: 'pass/fail status rendering for each story run' in AgentRuns AC is not verifiable by shell command — a grep or snapshot test would make it binary. Achievable 3: 5 pages + Keycloak auth integration + Vite build setup in one 3-point iteration is ambitious. TimeBound 4: well-bounded by specific file existence checks; the design phrase is the only scope-creep risk.

stories.title: Sovereign PM: Dockerfile and Helm chart
stories.description: Create sovereign-pm/Dockerfile (multi-stage: build frontend, build backend, combined production image). Create charts/sovereign-pm/ Helm chart deploying the PM app. PostgreSQL dependency (or external Crossplane-managed). Keycloak OIDC config from values. Ingress at pm.{{ .Values.global.domain }}. Ralph scripts mounted as a volume (reads prd.json from PVC shared with the K8s Job). ArgoCD app in argocd-apps/devex/sovereign-pm-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: sovereign-pm/Dockerfile builds successfully: docker build sovereign-pm/ exits 0
charts/sovereign-pm/ Helm chart passes helm lint
helm template charts/sovereign-pm/ | grep -i keycloak exits 0 (OIDC config present in rendered manifests)
Ingress resource rendered with host pm.{{ .Values.global.domain }}
PostgreSQL dependency declared in Chart.yaml (bitnami/postgresql subchart or Crossplane XRC reference)
charts/sovereign-pm/templates/ralph-job.yaml exists (Job template for on-demand Ralph runs)
argocd-apps/devex/sovereign-pm-app.yaml exists
helm template charts/sovereign-pm/ | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f - exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm lint charts/sovereign-pm/ exits 0; helm template charts/sovereign-pm/ | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f - exits 0; helm template charts/sovereign-pm/ | grep -i keycloak exits 0; helm template charts/sovereign-pm/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget exits 0; ls argocd-apps/devex/sovereign-pm-app.yaml exists; docker build sovereign-pm/ exits 0 (or docker build --no-cache if first run).

smart.notes: Specific 4: PostgreSQL dependency has two options ('bitnami/postgresql subchart OR Crossplane XRC reference') — implementer must choose; pick one in the story before implementing to avoid rework. TimeBound 4: the 'or' in the PostgreSQL dependency is the only scope ambiguity; all other ACs are binary and well-scoped.
